has_cut.description: True iff this clip contains one or more scene changes (cuts between distinct shots). A change of camera angle that is clearly cut-on-action within the SAME continuous shot does count as a cut here.

cut_at_s.description: If has_cut is true, the approximate timestamp within this clip (seconds from clip start, NOT source) where the cut occurs. Use the most prominent cut if multiple. If has_cut is false, return 0.

cut_kind.description: Type of cut. 'none' iff has_cut=false. 'hard' for instant frame-to-frame change. 'soft_dissolve' for cross-dissolves. 'fade' for fade-through-black. 'multiple' if more than one cut in this clip.

confidence.description: Confidence 0-1 that the has_cut classification is correct.

reasoning.description: One short sentence justifying the decision. Reference what changes at the cut point (location, lighting, characters, framing).
